# HPD.ML.Core

Generated from published HPD ML `0.5.0` packages.

## HPD-ML-Core

- [`AnnotationSet`](../types/hpd-ml-core-annotationset.md) - Undocumented in the published XML file.
- [`ArrayRowCursor`](../types/hpd-ml-core-arrayrowcursor.md) - Forward-only cursor over columnar arrays with column projection.
- [`CachedDataHandle`](../types/hpd-ml-core-cacheddatahandle.md) - Lazily materializes an inner IDataHandle on first access. Thread-safe via Lazy&lt;T&gt;.
- [`Column`](../types/hpd-ml-core-column.md) - Undocumented in the published XML file.
- [`ColumnCopyTransform`](../types/hpd-ml-core-columncopytransform.md) - Copies a column under a new name. Useful for preserving original values before a destructive transform.
- [`ColumnRenameTransform`](../types/hpd-ml-core-columnrenametransform.md) - Renames columns. Schema-only operation — no data movement.
- [`ColumnSelectTransform`](../types/hpd-ml-core-columnselecttransform.md) - Keeps or drops columns by name.
- [`ComposedTransform`](../types/hpd-ml-core-composedtransform.md) - Sequential composition of transforms. Produced by TransformComposer.Compose(). Schema propagation runs eagerly; data transformation runs lazily.
- [`ConcatenatedDataHandle`](../types/hpd-ml-core-concatenateddatahandle.md) - Vertical concatenation — appends rows from multiple DataHandles. Schemas must be vertically compatible (same columns, same types).
- [`CursorDataHandle`](../types/hpd-ml-core-cursordatahandle.md) - Wraps any cursor-producing function as a lazy IDataHandle. Used by data source adapters (CSV, Parquet, SQL) to provide lazy streaming without loading everything into memory.
- [`DataHandleSplitter`](../types/hpd-ml-core-datahandlesplitter.md) - Train/test splitting and cross-validation splitting. Returns views over the same underlying data — no copies.
- [`DefaultExecutionEnvironment`](../types/hpd-ml-core-defaultexecutionenvironment.md) - Default IExecutionEnvironment backed by HPD-Events. Provides logging, deterministic RNG, cancellation, and progress routing.
- [`DictionaryRow`](../types/hpd-ml-core-dictionaryrow.md) - Simple IRow backed by a dictionary. Used by transforms to produce output rows.
- [`FieldType`](../types/hpd-ml-core-fieldtype.md) - Undocumented in the published XML file.
- [`FilteredDataHandle`](../types/hpd-ml-core-filtereddatahandle.md) - Lazy row filtering. Does not touch data until cursored.
- [`InMemoryDataHandle`](../types/hpd-ml-core-inmemorydatahandle.md) - Columnar in-memory data. Supports cursor, async stream, and zero-copy tensor batch access. This is what Materialize() returns.
- [`LambdaTransform`](../types/hpd-ml-core-lambdatransform.md) - User-defined row transform via lambda. Escape hatch for one-off transformations. Not serializable. Not AOT-friendly (captures closure). Use sparingly.
- [`MappedCursor`](../types/hpd-ml-core-mappedcursor.md) - IRowCursor that applies a row mapping function to each row from an inner cursor.
- [`Model`](../types/hpd-ml-core-model.md) - Default IModel implementation. Immutable bundle of a scoring transform and the learned parameters that produced it.
- [`ProgressSubject`](../types/hpd-ml-core-progresssubject.md) - Bridges IObservable&lt;ProgressEvent&gt; to HPD-Events. Learner implementations use this to emit progress events that are both observable via Rx and routed through the event coordinator.
- [`Row`](../types/hpd-ml-core-row.md) - Row backed by columnar arrays at a specific row index.
- [`Schema`](../types/hpd-ml-core-schema.md) - Immutable schema with explicit merge semantics. Constructed via SchemaBuilder or directly from a column list.
- [`SchemaBuilder`](../types/hpd-ml-core-schemabuilder.md) - Fluent builder for constructing schemas.
- [`SeededRandom`](../types/hpd-ml-core-seededrandom.md) - Deterministic RNG helper. Given a seed, produces reproducible sequences.
- [`ShuffledDataHandle`](../types/hpd-ml-core-shuffleddatahandle.md) - Randomized row ordering. Materializes on first access (must know all rows to shuffle).
- [`TensorHelpers`](../types/hpd-ml-core-tensorhelpers.md) - Bridges between columnar arrays and ReadOnlyTensorSpan&lt;T&gt;. Used by InMemoryDataHandle and external engine adapters.
- [`TrainingProgressEvent`](../types/hpd-ml-core-trainingprogressevent.md) - HPD-Events wrapper for training progress.
- [`TransformedDataHandle`](../types/hpd-ml-core-transformeddatahandle.md) - Lazy result of applying an ITransform to an IDataHandle. Does not touch data until a cursor, stream, or batch is requested.
